mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS (0.09% as of July 2013) reflects an accommodative liquidity regime that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, sustaining positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the rate remains pinned near the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.09 in July 2013, unchanged for multiple months
regime_hint: zero lower bound / extended monetary easing
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in UNRATE that crosses below its trailing 6-month moving average by more than 0.1pp signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises consensus expectations of consumer income growth and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.1pp below its trailing 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 7.6 to 7.4 between May and July 2013
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time, or the effect disappears (p>0.10) once controlling for baseline unconditional positive-return rate of 65.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in HOUST (housing starts) and RSAFS (retail sales) indicates simultaneous strengthening of both durable-goods investment and consumer spending, a combination that historically signals broadening aggregate demand and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone carries the same signal because housing starts can rise on supply factors while retail sales can be driven by price inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 839 (revised) and RSAFS at 410268 in mid-2013 both trending upward
failure_condition: Joint HOUST+RSAFS 3-month growth above their historical medians fails to produce 20-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional base rate by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output capacity utilization, which is incorporated quickly by equity markets as a signal of near-term earnings strength, producing modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=99.0814 (revised) in July 2013, up from prior months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO prints are not significantly different from returns following flat/negative prints (t-test p>0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a transition period where markets anticipate the Federal Reserve tapering asset purchases (as evidenced by rising long-term yield expectations despite FEDFUNDS remaining at the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL showing subdued inflation), equity markets experience short-horizon negative returns as risk premia reprice upward ahead of policy normalization signals.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% while market commentary/policy signals indicate imminent QE taper (regime flag, not derivable from FEDFUNDS level alone)
evidence_refs: fact:2013 Q2-Q3 taper tantrum period with FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL=232.9 (revised)
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during the identified taper-anticipation window are not significantly negative (p>0.05), or fail to underperform the unconditional 5-day base rate by more than 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive PAYEMS growth and declining UNRATE jointly confirm genuine labor market tightening rather than a statistical artifact of labor force exits, and this combined signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon because it reflects durable consumer income growth supporting corporate revenues.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=136391 (revised) rising and UNRATE=7.3 (revised) falling in mid-2013
failure_condition: Instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds its 6-month average AND UNRATE declines simultaneously fail to produce 60-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional base rate at p>0.05 significance over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth crosses above its trailing 12-month average by more than 1%, it signals a discrete acceleration in consumer spending that historically leads to upward revisions in corporate earnings guidance, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth exceeds trailing 12-month average growth by more than 1 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=410268 (revised) in July 2013 showing steady consumer spending growth
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show 20-day forward positive-return rate below 60% (near or below the unconditional 65.5% base rate), or Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where INDPRO growth decelerates for three consecutive months while CPIAUCSL remains stable, the slowdown signals approaching capacity constraints or waning demand momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as markets price in slower forward earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO growth pace moderate at 99.0814 (revised) in July 2013 amid low CPI inflation
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating industrial output
failure_condition: During INDPRO deceleration episodes lasting 3+ months, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not significantly negative (p>0.05) relative to unconditional base rate, falsifying the mechanism.
